post_paper_additions: 47 SKILL.md files plus skill loader/agent support
ConsensusEval modules, open-ended runner, reference-model template, and tutorial

null_or_zero_reference_notes.interpretation: The numerical evaluator can inspect source files and optional rubrics, so a null static reference is not automatically unscorable; it does increase dependence on the configured evaluator agent. QA_18, QA_23, and QA_50 have neither a static reference nor a rubric.

chart_notes.interpretation: DV_84 embeds its data in the instruction and is not necessarily defective. DV_90 provides no correctness rubric, weakening the correctness branch while leaving visual scoring possible.

broken_file_generation_reference.effect: The standard evaluator constructs the manifest reference path and therefore cannot load the released Chinese reference without manual correction. The English counterpart has a differently named _res.csv reference.

evaluation_topology.QA: Configured code-capable model receives task, optional reference/rubric, and source files; it emits a 0/1 judgment and reason. There is no deterministic answer parser or released human calibration set.
evaluation_topology.data_visualization: One configured multimodal model separately judges rubric correctness from the image and visual quality from the image, each as passed/total; final score is 0.7 correctness + 0.3 readability.
evaluation_topology.file_generation: A code-capable Claude evaluator samples/compares candidate and reference workbooks and returns one 0-100 score; the main runner uses one pass. There is no deterministic full-cell comparison or released evaluator calibration set.
evaluation_topology.open_ended_consensus: Post-paper extension: reference agents analyze data, an LLM semantically clusters findings, non-consensus findings are cross-validated by resumed reference sessions, a generated 50/30/20 rubric is created, and another Hermes agent judges the candidate. It is absent from the paper-time archive, the 603-task dataset, and the paper results.

static_verification.reason_for_no_end_to_end_score_reproduct: The paper does not release immutable model outputs, evaluator outputs, task-level score matrices, seeds, retry ledgers, model endpoint snapshots, or a table-reproduction script. Re-running would require mutable paid endpoints and would not recreate paper-time responses.

claim_ceiling.released_package_conformance: partially supported; 603 unique bilingual task IDs and almost all manifest paths are inspectable, but FG_82 has a broken Chinese reference path and paper/current snapshots differ
claim_ceiling.artifact_correctness: partially supported only at configured evaluator level; the evidence views are lossy and evaluator accuracy is uncalibrated
claim_ceiling.analytical_quality: not established beyond task-local model-judge scores
claim_ceiling.professional_validity: not established; no downstream user acceptance/use study or professional threshold
claim_ceiling.configured_system_capability: descriptive for the paper-time model-plus-scaffold-plus-tool package only
claim_ceiling.procurement_utility: not established; no reliability, latency, retry, total-cost, or stakeholder-loss model

scope_boundary: This was a static paper, source, manifest, and evaluator audit. No untrusted task workload or paid evaluator/model call was run.
